sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root קָדַשׁ), with Dag. euph. מִקְּדָשׁ Exo 15:17, with suff. מִקְדָּשֹׁו, once (unusually) מִקְדְּשֹׁו Num 18:29.

(1) a holy thing, something consecrated, Num. loc. cit.

(2) a sanctuary, a holy place; specially used of the holy tabernacle of the Israelites, Exo 25:8 Lev 12:4 21:12 Num 10:21 18:1 of the temple, 1Ch 22:19 2Ch 29:21. Often more fully, מְקֹום מִקְדַּשׁ יְיָ Isa 60:13 מְכֹון מִקְדַּשׁ יְיָ Dan 8:11. מִקְדַּשׁ מֶלֶךְ a sacred place which a king has, i.e. consecrated by him, Amo 7:13. Plur. מִקְדְּשֵׁי בֵּית יְיָ Jer 51:51, the sanctuaries, the holy places of the temple; מִקְדְּשֵׁי אֵל Psa 73:17 id.; but מִקְדְשֵׁי יִשְׂרָאֵל are the sanctuaries of Israel (Gentile and prohibited), Amo 7:9.

(3) an dsylum, since temples amongst the Hebrews, as amongst the Greeks, had the right of asylum, Isa 8:14 Eze 11:16 (compare 1Ki 1:50 2:28 ).
